headline: Occidental Balances Debt Buybacks Carbon Capture Plans And Berkshire’s Growing Stake
summary: Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) launched cash tender offers to repurchase portions of its outstanding senior notes and debentures. Berkshire Hathaway has been building an already large position in Occidental, leading to a more concentrated shareholder base. For you as an investor, Occidental sits at the intersection of conventional hydrocarbons and emerging...

headline: Oil Stocks Heat Up Amid Iran War As S&P 500 Slips; How High Will Oil Prices Go?
summary: Oil stocks crowded the top of the S&P 500 early Monday as the U.S.-Israel war against Iran entered its third day.  The war is damaging Iran's oil export infrastructure and snarling tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.  Supply worry boosted the U.S. crude oil price 6.4% to $71.30 a barrel in the futures market.

summary: Oil prices could move higher still if Iran targets Persian Gulf energy infrastructure and the Strait of Hormuz closure is prolonged.

headline: The Worst Case Scenario for Oil Is Here. $100 Crude Could Be Coming.
summary: The war in Iran has led to a worst-case scenario for the  oil  market: the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil flows.  A prolonged closure could send international oil prices above $100 a barrel, a level they haven’t hit since 2022.  On Saturday, about 4 million barrels of crude made it through—virtually all of Iranian origin, according to JP Morgan strategist Natasha Kaneva.

raw.headline: The Worst Case Scenario for Oil Is Here. $100 Crude Could Be Coming.
raw.summary: The war in Iran has led to a worst-case scenario for the  oil  market: the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil flows.  A prolonged closure could send international oil prices above $100 a barrel, a level they haven’t hit since 2022.  On Saturday, about 4 million barrels of crude made it through—virtually all of Iranian origin, according to JP Morgan strategist Natasha Kaneva.

headline: Why Berkshire Hathaway’s Earnings Weren’t as Bad as They Looked
summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter operating earnings weren’t great, but they were better than the headline decline of 30%.  Adjust for a $1.56 billion noncash goodwill impairment loss related to four of Berkshire’s subsidiaries, and some other one-time factors, and the drop in operating profits would have been closer to 20%, Barron’s estimates.  Berkshire included that after-tax impairment in its “Other” Income segment of operating income even as it excluded a $4.5 billion noncash write-down of its equity stake in  Occidental Petroleum  from its fourth-quarter operating income.

raw.headline: Why Berkshire Hathaway’s Earnings Weren’t as Bad as They Looked
raw.summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter operating earnings weren’t great, but they were better than the headline decline of 30%.  Adjust for a $1.56 billion noncash goodwill impairment loss related to four of Berkshire’s subsidiaries, and some other one-time factors, and the drop in operating profits would have been closer to 20%, Barron’s estimates.  Berkshire included that after-tax impairment in its “Other” Income segment of operating income even as it excluded a $4.5 billion noncash write-down of its equity stake in  Occidental Petroleum  from its fourth-quarter operating income.

headline: Oil’s Worst Case Scenario Is Here. $100 Crude Could Be Coming.
summary: The war in Iran has led to a worst-case scenario for the  oil  market: the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil flows.  On Saturday, about 4 million barrels of crude made it through—virtually all of Iranian origin, according to JP Morgan strategist Natasha Kaneva.  Wood Mackenzie analyst Alan Gelder thinks it’s plausible that oil flows are disrupted for weeks.
